name: safe-credit-solutions

certifications: BBB rating: 1/5 stars from customer reviews
Multiple complaints about unauthorized charges and cancellation difficulties

best_for: Consumers in the Miami area seeking Spanish-English bilingual credit repair services
Individuals with credit report errors who want local, in-person credit repair consultation
People who have thoroughly researched the company and verified current pricing before enrolling
description_short: Safe Credit Solutions is a Miami-based credit repair firm. 4.9 Google rating from 2,900+ reviews BUT BBB shows 1/5 stars with serious complaints about unauthorized charges and difficulty canceling services.
description_long:
Safe Credit Solutions Inc. is a credit repair firm based in Miami, Florida, offering services to dispute inaccurate items on consumer credit reports. The company has built a substantial Google review profile with approximately 2,900 reviews at a 4.9-star rating, suggesting strong client acquisition in the South Florida market. However, the company's reputation across other platforms tells a starkly different story that prospective clients must understand before engaging.

The company's services reportedly include credit report analysis across all three bureaus, dispute letter preparation and filing, debt validation requests, and credit-building guidance. Safe Credit Solutions markets primarily to the South Florida Hispanic community with bilingual (English/Spanish) services. The specific pricing structure is not publicly available, though complaints suggest monthly subscription fees.

The BBB profile for Safe Credit Solutions shows a 1-star average from 6 customer reviews — among the worst on the platform. BBB complaints describe a troubling pattern: consumers report being charged monthly with no results, extreme difficulty canceling services, unresponsive customer service, and continued billing even after explicit cancellation requests. One complaint describes the company asserting to a bank that a consumer was still active despite documented cancellation, with charges continuing for over a year. These allegations — unauthorized charges, cancellation resistance, and phantom billing — are among the most serious in the credit repair industry and mirror patterns associated with subscription traps.

In the broader ecosystem of credit repair services, consumers have multiple paths to improving their credit. Professional credit repair companies can dispute inaccurate items with all three bureaus, while credit monitoring services provide ongoing alerts about changes to your reports. For those building credit from scratch, secured credit cards and credit builder loans offer structured approaches. Consumers dealing with overwhelming debt may benefit from debt consolidation loans to simplify payments, or credit counseling through nonprofit agencies for personalized budgeting guidance.
diagnosis: Safe Credit Solutions presents a significant red flag pattern: high Google reviews (4.9 stars, 2,900+) combined with very low BBB ratings (1 star) and complaints about unauthorized charges, difficult cancellations, and continued billing after cancellation. This disconnect between Google ratings and BBB complaints is a common pattern in companies that aggressively solicit positive Google reviews while having fundamental service delivery or billing problems. Consumers should exercise extreme caution. Before enrolling: verify pricing in writing, understand cancellation procedures, monitor credit card statements closely, and consider established national firms like The Credit People or Lexington Law instead.
pros: Offers credit repair services across all three major credit bureaus
Free initial consultation available for prospective clients
Located in Miami, FL providing accessibility for South Florida consumers
cons: BBB consumer rating of only 1 out of 5 stars is a significant red flag
Multiple consumer complaints describe unauthorized charges and billing issues
BBB notes on Miami entity suggest potential business status concerns
Limited transparency about pricing, dispute processes, and success rates
Consumer complaints filed with multiple regulatory agencies
services: Three-bureau credit report analysis and review
Debt validation requests to collection agencies
Financial education and credit building guidance
